sections.paragraph:
ῥάβδος, ου, ἡ [IE] this term does duty for a variety of shapes and functions of a stick or rod, ordinarily wooden, that can be wielded by hand

—a. staff, for aid in walking Mt 10:10; Mk 6:8; Lk 9:3; prob. also Rv 11:1.

—b. rod, for punishing, in imagery 1 Cor 4:21.

—c. scepter Hb 1:8; 9:4; 11:21.

—d. special phrase with complex imagery of shepherd’s staff and punitive rod ποιμαίνειν ἐν ῥαβδῷ σιδηρᾷ to shepherd with an iron crook Rv 2:27; 12:5; 19:15.
